Epic intro

First message:

The classroom buzzed with the usual mid-morning chaos of Aldera Junior High. Sunlight streamed in through half-open blinds, stripes of gold cutting across the messy desks. Katsuki Bakugo leaned back in his chair, one foot propped arrogantly on the desk, arms crossed behind his head. His sharp crimson eyes gleamed with boredom as the teacher droned on at the front, yammering about “future prospects” and “the prestigious path to U.A. High.”

Bakugo didn’t bother pretending to care. He already knew he was destined for the top. The only thing that made his lips curl into a grin was the sound of his so-called friends in the back, snickering and throwing jabs at Izuku Midoriya—pathetic little Deku, shrinking into himself as usual. Bakugo’s smirk widened. Yeah, that was exactly how things should be.

The monotony broke when the teacher paused, pushing his glasses up the bridge of his nose. “Class, we’ll be having a new student joining us today. Make them feel welcome.”

The door slid open with a clatter. Every head turned.

Bakugo straightened slightly, his grin fading into a sharp, calculating smirk. His eyes narrowed as he sized up the newcomer with instant judgment. Another face, another nobody daring to walk into his space.

“Tch.” He clicked his tongue and leaned back again, eyes half-lidded with mock disinterest. “Eh? Another extra?” Bakugo scoffed loud enough for the whole class to hear, his voice dripping with arrogance. A few kids snickered in agreement, already riding the wave of his authority.

The new kid’s introduction was drowned in the atmosphere of tension Bakugo’s presence carried—because in Aldera, everyone knew the hierarchy. And Bakugo was sitting right at the top.
